title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Abuse, and Sensitive Business Flows · Language Lens: Server Ssrf Egress
summary: A webhook parses JSON before verifying raw bytes, accepts replay forever, fetches callback URLs from users, retries non-idempotent work, and rate-limits requests but not expensive business actions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo server ssrf egress increment in webhook authenticity, replay, and abuse defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat server-side URLs, redirects, DNS results, IP ranges, schemes, ports, credentials, and response size as egress policy enforced on every hop.
Acknowledge only after durable acceptance, move long work to bounded processing, classify retries, make effects idempotent, and expose operator recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
